Title: A first-in-class dimethyl 2-acetamido terephthalate inhibitor targeting Conyza canadensis SHMT1 with a novel herbicidal mode-of-action

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Horseweed / Canadian fleabane; transgenic rice for validation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild Conyza canadensis; Nipponbare (for overexpression validation)
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Photorespiration / one-carbon metabolism linked amino acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Serine–glycine interconversion in the photorespiratory cycle
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream, essential step in photorespiratory amino acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports photorespiration and one-carbon metabolism essential for plant growth; SHMT also contributes to H2O2 scavenging and abiotic stress tolerance as discussed in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Chemical inhibition of CcSHMT1 by compound 9ay causes herbicidal injury in Conyza canadensis and other weeds; overexpression of CcSHMT1 confers increased resistance to 9ay and CAP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: X-ray crystal structure determination; in silico docking; recombinant protein expression and in vitro enzyme inhibition assay; transmission electron microscopy of mitochondria; transgenic overexpression in rice (Nipponbare); RT-qPCR; in vivo SHMT activity assay; H2O2 measurement; herbicidal bioassays and field trial
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PLP cofactor; inhibitor-binding residues Ser146; Pro169; Tyr178; Lys410; PLP-contact residues Asp253; His281; Lys282; Gly327
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CcSHMT1 was structurally resolved at 2.8 Å and shown to be a conserved α-class PLP-dependent SHMT tetramer. 2. Virtual screening and synthesis identified dimethyl 2-acetamido terephthalate derivative 9ay as a potent herbicidal inhibitor targeting CcSHMT1. 3. 9ay inhibited recombinant CcSHMT1 activity in vitro, damaged mitochondria in treated leaves, and CcSHMT1 overexpression in rice reduced sensitivity to 9ay and CAP, confirming target engagement. 4. 9ay showed strong weed control with low visible side effect on maize and low honeybee toxicity.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Treatment with compound 9ay, CAP, or 2,4-D; greenhouse and field herbicide assays; post-emergence and pre-emergence conditions
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pCambia2301-based transgene in rice; specific promoter not stated
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a molecular target for herbicide discovery rather than nutrient-quality breeding; SHMT structure-guided design may enable selective weed control and engineering of herbicide tolerance via SHMT1 overexpression or allele modification in crops
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Soybean and Hibiscus cannabinus were strongly affected; slight phytotoxicity to rice and maize at higher dosage reported for 9ao/9ay; broad conservation of PLP enzymes raises potential off-target effects across related enzymes/species
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protein crystal structure deposited as PDB 7E13. The study focuses on herbicide mode of action, not nutritional enhancement. Terminal metabolite is pathway product inferred from known SHMT biochemistry, whereas the experimentally measured phenotype is weed growth inhibition rather than metabolite quantification.
